items.title: Element of Design — A Visual Atlas
items.description:
A four-part study on the building blocks of visual design.

**Pareto Principle** — the 80/20 rule mapped onto how a viewer's eye actually scans a layout: 41% top-left, 20% top-right, 25% middle-left, 14% middle-right.

**Rule of Thirds** — placing the focal object at one of the four intersections rather than dead-centre.

**Occam's Razor** — among competing solutions, the simplest one (the one with the fewest assumptions) is usually the best. Demonstrated on a clean app settings screen.

**Elements & Design Matrix** — a personal grid of line, shape, form, point, colour, texture, value and space, paired with a separate matrix mapping those elements against the principles (balance, rhythm, emphasis, contrast, movement, harmony, proportion).

items.title: Miniature Portrait Study
items.description: A hand-painted study in the tradition of Indian miniature painting — a woman in profile, draped in saffron and gold, framed by a deep teal field and a striped indigo-and-gold border. Sequins, beadwork and metallic ink stand in for the kundan and meenakari work of the originals; the gesture of the brushwork keeps the scale intimate. A study of how an entire history of court painting compresses into a single face.

items.title: The Automatic Air Freshener — A Comic
items.description: A four-panel storytelling exercise. A boy boasts that his new automatic air freshener will turn his bedroom into a garden of flowers. Just past midnight, as he settles into a peaceful ambience, the device hisses awake — *Psshhhttt* — and a ghost rides out on the mist. "You scared the life out of me!" Storytelling, panel pacing, and reading the moment a small product becomes a small horror story.

items.title: User Journey Map — Buying Spectacles
items.description:
An end-to-end map of the experience of buying and living with a new pair of spectacles, from the moment the box arrives to long-term ownership. Seven phases (seeing the box → opening → removing content → reading instructions → first use → everyday experience → long-term ownership), an emotion line that swings from excitement to nose-pad-sweat frustration to good response, and three lanes of insight: pain points, moments of delight, and opportunities (eco-friendly packaging, frustration-free seals, QR-coded video guides, a free six-monthly cleaning subscription, 24/7 multilingual customer care).

Goals captured: clear vision, comfort & fit, style & appearance, durability & quality, convenience & service, confidence & trust.
